title: reseda lutea subsp. gracilis
intro: Reseda lutea subsp. gracilis, also known as the slender weld, is a subspecies of the yellow weld plant, a member of the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from coughs and colds. Reseda lutea subsp. gracilis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has also been used in traditional medicine to aid in the treatment of various ailments.
